title: Patch<T> doc falsely claims no struct uses derive(Serialize) with Patch fields
description: The Patch<T> doc comment says 'In practice, all structs in this crate build their patch map manually via Patch::map_entry rather than deriving Serialize, which avoids the attribute requirement entirely.' This is false: ChatPresenceEvent in types.rs is #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)] and has two Patch<String> fields, correctly guarded by #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Patch::is_keep")]. The false claim causes a correctness hazard: any future contributor adding a derive(Serialize) struct with Patch<T> fields may omit the required skip_serializing_if attribute based on this guidance, producing a runtime serde error on first serialization of a Keep-valued field.
close_reason: Fixed: Patch<T> doc updated to cover both patterns; RFC citation corrected to §3.4; tests added for duplicate call_id and duplicate accountId
